channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Yanga Footballers Donate Desks and Supplies to Improve School Infrastructure
item.description: Three prominent Yanga Sports Club players, Dickson Job, Kibwana Shomary, and Abuutwalib Mshery, recently visited Kilonga Primary School to donate essential educational supplies. The initiative include...

item.title: Andy Burnham poised to become UK Prime Minister after securing Labour leadership nomination
item.description: Andy Burnham has secured the majority of Labour MPs' support, with 322 nominations, making him the sole candidate for Labour leadership. This positions him to become the next UK Prime Minister, follow...

item.title: Baguio Courts Urge Face Masks to Curb COVID-19 Spread
item.description: The Baguio Regional Trial Court (RTC) and Municipal Trial Court in Cities (MTCC) have issued a face mask advisory for all individuals entering the Hall of Justice after a court employee tested positiv...

item.title: Fremantle Dockers Deliver Stunning Comeback Against Sydney Swans in AFL Clash
item.description: In a thrilling AFL match, the Fremantle Dockers staged a remarkable second-half comeback to defeat the Sydney Swans 15.21 (111) to 10.13 (73). Trailing by 25 points at the end of the second quarter, t...

item.title: Live-Action 'Moana' Remake Faces Criticism for Lack of Innovation
item.description: The live-action adaptation of Disney's *Moana* has sparked debate over its necessity, with critics arguing it lacks originality despite its high budget and A-list cast. The film follows the Polynesian...

item.title: Tragic deaths of a man and his four-year-old nephew linked to contaminated biscuits from a tuckshop
item.description: Police in Witbank are investigating the deaths of a 43-year-old man and his four-year-old nephew, who allegedly died after consuming biscuits from a looted tuckshop. The incident occurred on 8 July 20...

item.title: Prime Minister Carney Reacts to Diplomatic Gift from Turkish President Erdogan
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney expressed surprise upon receiving an engraved revolver as a gift from Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan during the NATO Summit in Ankara. The weapon, individually inscr...

item.title: Brianna LaPaglia's sisters: Family dynamics and the influencer's journey
item.description: The article explores the life of Brianna LaPaglia, a social media influencer known for her viral TikTok content and podcasting career. It highlights her three half-sisters from her father's first marr...

item.title: Judge Rejects Trump DOJ's Attempt to Ban Olympian from Reflecting Pool
item.description: A D.C. judge rejected a government request to bar three-time Olympian David Hearn from the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool after he pleaded not guilty to a felony destruction of property charge. U.S....

item.title: Canada Condemns Iran's Attacks on Ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Canadian Foreign Affairs Minister Anita Anand condemned Iran's recent attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz as 'unjustified' and 'unacceptable,' emphasizing that such actions violate a c...

item.title: Research shows how racial stereotypes continue to shape the development and transfer of African footballers
item.description: The article examines how long-standing racial stereotypes continue to influence the global football industry, especially in the recruitment and development of African footballers. Against the backdrop...

item.title: How a Sleep Expert's Diet Enhances Nighttime Rest
item.description: Research by sleep professor Marie-Pierre St-Onge highlights how dietary choices significantly impact sleep quality. Her studies show that a Mediterranean-style diet, rich in fruits, vegetables, nuts, ...

item.title: Vitamin A and thyroid hormones regulate human central vision development
item.description: Researchers have uncovered how vitamin A derivatives and thyroid hormone signaling coordinate to establish the specialized photoreceptor cells in the fovea, which enable sharp color vision. This break...

item.title: Indian ships navigate Strait of Hormuz amid escalating US-Iran tensions
item.description: Two Indian ships transporting crude oil successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway, amid heightened tensions between the US and Iran. The vessels, flagged under India's national ...

item.title: Bettor from Camarines Sur Wins P15 Million Lotto Jackpot
item.description: A bettor from Camarines Sur has won the jackpot in the 6/45 Mega Lotto draw, according to the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO). The winning combination, 13-05-30-25-22-11, was correctly gu...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Business Training Initiative to Empower Entrepreneurs
item.description: A new business training initiative, organized by Coprosperity Fund, aims to address critical gaps faced by Tanzanian entrepreneurs. The Small Business Seminar Series targets entrepreneurs across six r...

item.title: Extended Heat Health Alerts as UK Records 34C Days in a Year
item.description: Amber heat health alerts have been extended across much of England, with temperatures reaching 35C during the third heatwave of the year. The Met Office confirmed this heatwave marks the eighth day th...

item.title: World Bank Pushes Kenya for Anti-Corruption Measures to Secure Loan Package
item.description: The World Bank has urged Kenya to implement strict anti-corruption reforms as a condition for approving its $750bn lending package. The bank lowered Kenya’s 2026 economic growth projection to 4.3% due...

item.title: Auditor-General's Report Says Ondo SUBEB Failed to Address Audit Queries and Submit Contract Records Over Five Years
item.description: The 2025 report of the Ondo State Auditor-General says the Ondo State Universal Basic Education Board (SUBEB) has repeatedly failed to respond to audit queries, obey legislative directives and provide...

item.title: Iran's Conflict Escalates as Supreme Leader Khamenei Laid to Rest Amid Ceasefire Tensions
item.description: The article details the burial of Iran's slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on July 9, 2026, following U.S. airstrikes on February 28. The event coincided with Iranian military strikes on U.S...
